_source: Extracted from developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/function-calling and the Tools guide. This is the actual schema format OpenAI requires for function calling in the Responses API and the Agents SDK.

strict_mode.behavior: When strict: true, the model is constrained at decode time to emit only JSON that conforms to the schema. This is enforced via OpenAI's structured outputs mechanism, not as post-hoc validation.
requirements: additionalProperties must equal false for each object
All fields in properties must be marked required
Optional fields use type: ['string', 'null'] (union with null)
Some advanced JSON Schema features remain unsupported
strict_mode.default_under_responses_api: When strict is omitted, the Responses API normalizes the schema into strict mode automatically. Chat Completions remains non-strict by default.

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Retrieves current weather for the given location.

location.description: City and country e.g. Bogotá, Colombia

units.description: Units the temperature will be returned in.

tools.name: issue_refund
tools.description: Issue a refund against an order.
tools._note: Destructiveness, idempotency, and approval requirements are NOT first-class fields in OpenAI's tool schema — they're application-layer conventions. The schema itself enforces only argument validity.

tools._doc: Includes current internet data in model responses. Model automatically decides whether to invoke based on prompt context.
tools._sandboxing: Runs in OpenAI's infrastructure; results returned to model as content blocks

tools._doc: Searches uploaded file contents to provide context during response generation.

tools._sandboxing: Hosted shell + Code Interpreter run in isolated container sessions, billed at the 20-minute session rate per OpenAI pricing
tools._doc: Executes Python in a sandboxed environment. Useful for computation, file I/O on uploaded files, plotting.
Enables agentic workflows where models control computer interfaces. Higher-risk tool — explicit safety analysis in Operator system card.
Remote Model Context Protocol server access. The require_approval setting is OpenAI's first-class human-in-the-loop gate for external MCP tools.

validation_layer.strategy: Structured outputs constrain the model's token-level decoding to produce only schema-conforming JSON. When strict is enabled, schema-invalid output is structurally impossible for the model to emit.
what_this_protects_against: Hallucinated parameter types (string for int, etc.)
Extra unexpected fields when additionalProperties: false
what_this_does_NOT_protect_against: Semantically wrong but schema-valid arguments (e.g. valid order_id, wrong order)
Foreign-key non-existence (calling with an ID that does not exist in the buyer's system)

parallel_tool_calls.control: Set parallel_tool_calls: false to restrict to zero or one tool call per turn.
parallel_tool_calls.implication: Multiple tools can fire in parallel by default. Buyer's tool handlers must be designed for concurrent execution OR the developer must opt out per-call.

tool_choice._doc: Explicit guidance on which tools the model can invoke; can force a specific function or restrict to a subset

approval_gate.function_calls: No built-in per-function approval gate. Developer must implement at the application layer or via the Agents SDK's input/output guardrails.
approval_gate.agents_sdk_pattern: InputGuardrail / OutputGuardrail with tripwire_triggered halts execution before or after a tool call.

tool_search._doc: Dynamically loads tool definitions at runtime via defer_loading flag. Lets the model select from large tool catalogs without loading all definitions upfront.
tool_search._implication: Reduces context-token cost for vendors with many tools.
